patches.description: An automated method identifies quiet-time suprathermal background by fitting two power laws to the variance-mean relationship of ACE/ULEIS 24-hour intervals; their intersection defines the threshold with a 1-sigma uncertainty.
patches.voice_grounding_source: summary_short field; technical_caption; figure-detail voice (declarative finding, technical specifics)

patches.description: The variance-mean threshold cleanly separates active and quiet intervals in a five-day July 2012 time series, with only 7 of 29 quiet intervals falling inside the 1-sigma uncertainty band.
patches.voice_grounding_source: summary_short field; the_finding paragraph; figure-detail voice

patches.description: Quiet-time thresholds derived from ACE/ULEIS variance-mean fits cluster within a narrow band across two solar cycles (1997-2021), confirming the method captures a real physical transition rather than year-specific noise.
patches.voice_grounding_source: summary_short field; the_finding paragraph; figure-detail voice

patches.description: Annual quiet-hour counts anticorrelate with sunspot number at Pearson rho = -0.95 across 1997-2021: solar maximum leaves few quiet intervals; solar minimum is dominated by them.
patches.voice_grounding_source: summary_short field; the_finding paragraph; figure-detail voice

patches.description: Quiet-time heavy-ion abundances normalized to oxygen shift systematically with the solar cycle: low-FIP species (Mg, Si, S, Ca, Fe) correlate with sunspot number above +0.8 while carbon anticorrelates at -0.84.
patches.voice_grounding_source: summary_short field; the_finding paragraph; figure-detail voice

patches.description: Dividing quiet-time abundances by CIR reference values flattens the M/Q trend during solar minimum; dividing by gradual-SEP values flattens it during maximum. Impulsive-SEP normalization fails in both phases.
patches.voice_grounding_source: summary_short field; the_finding paragraph; figure-detail voice

patches.description: Eight heavy-ion abundances follow power-law trends with Fe/C, the single-number enrichment indicator. Year-coded data traces cyclic loops tied to solar-cycle phase rather than scattering randomly.
patches.voice_grounding_source: summary_short field; the_finding paragraph; figure-detail voice

patches.description: Power-law exponents for X/O versus Fe/C run from C (-0.21) through Ne (-0.02), Mg (+0.36), Si (+0.47), S (+0.54), Ca (+0.61), to Fe (+0.79), holding roughly constant across four solar-cycle extrema.
patches.voice_grounding_source: summary_short field; numerical values in the_finding paragraph; figure-detail voice

patches.description: Abundance variability rises systematically with M/Q across all four solar-cycle extrema, with slopes matching the gradual-SEP trend Desai et al. (2006) measured in individual events.
patches.voice_grounding_source: summary_short field; the_finding paragraph; technical_caption; figure-detail voice

patches.description: The slope of M/Q fractionation is statistically consistent across all four solar-cycle extrema and matches the Desai et al. (2006) gradual-SEP value; threshold-choice variability stays well inside the fit uncertainty.
patches.voice_grounding_source: summary_short field; the_finding paragraph; figure-detail voice

patches.description: Sunspot-number correlation of quiet-time abundances divides cleanly at FIP = 11 eV, not at M/Q. Sulfur (FIP = 10.4 eV) lands decisively on the low-FIP side, contradicting prior treatment of S as a high-FIP species.
patches.voice_grounding_source: summary_short field; the_finding paragraph; technical_caption; figure-detail voice

patches.description: Nine heavy-ion species from carbon (mass 12) to iron (mass 56) follow nearly identical power-law spectra over 0.3 to 1.28 MeV/nucleon in 2004, with no breaks or rollovers across the full energy range.
patches.voice_grounding_source: summary_short field; the_finding paragraph; figure-detail voice

patches.description: The oxygen spectral index fluctuates around 2.5 across 1998-2020 without tracking the dramatic swings in sunspot number; only two of nine species show even a modest correlation with solar activity.
patches.voice_grounding_source: summary_short field; the_finding paragraph; figure-detail voice

patches.description: Carbon spectral indices cluster tightly along the one-to-one diagonal with oxygen (rank correlation 0.93); iron follows with more scatter, and the two species bracket the full M/Q range studied.
patches.voice_grounding_source: summary_short field; the_finding paragraph; figure-detail voice

patches.description: Pooling 150+ spectral-slope measurements across nine species and 22 years yields gamma = 2.5 +/- 0.3, ruling out the 1.5 prediction from continuous compressional-heating models as the sole mechanism.
patches.voice_grounding_source: summary_short field; the_finding paragraph; figure-detail voice
